steps.title: Identify the clauses needing connection
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses at the blank. The first clause, “That the geographic center of North America lay in the state of North Dakota was conceded by all involved,” is a complete thought with main verb “was conceded.” The second clause, “establishing its precise coordinates proved more divisive,” is also complete with main verb “proved.”

steps.title: Apply the independent clause rule
steps.content: A semicolon joins two independent clauses. A comma alone creates a comma splice (B), no punctuation creates a run-on sentence (C), and a colon is for elaboration or explanation, not a contrasting point (A). This matches choice D — D is correct.
